Data Sourcing and Generation Steps:

1. SOURCE DATA DOWNLOAD
   Source: Federal Reserve Board, H.10 Foreign Exchange Rates, historical
   release pages (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/hist/) --
   one page per currency, each holding that currency's full multi-year
   daily series (so one download per currency covers any number of
   requested dates -- no per-date network round-trip).
   Script: tools/fetch_fed_h10_rates.py
   Command:
     ./tools/fetch_fed_h10_rates.py --currency eu,uk,sz,ja,sd,al,ca,nz,sf,mx,in \
         --dates 2016-02-05 --format table \
         --archive-zip external/fed_h10/pages.zip --force
   Target: external/fed_h10/pages.zip (raw HTML pages) +
     external/fed_h10/manifest.json (archive provenance, written alongside
     the zip, not inside it)

2. PARSE
   The same script parses each page's fixed-format rows
   (<th id="r1">DD-MON-YY</th> ... <td>value</td>) into (date, value)
   pairs. Rows whose value is "ND" (no data, e.g. a bank holiday) are
   skipped -- never interpolated or filled by us.

3. GENERATE SQL
   Command:
     ./tools/fetch_fed_h10_rates.py --currency eu,uk,sz,ja,sd,al,ca,nz,sf,mx,in \
         --dates <25 target dates, see marketdata_fx_driver_rates_populate.sql> \
         --format sql
   Output: SQL VALUES rows, each citing its own currency's Fed release
   page as source_url -- pasted directly into
   projects/ores.sql/populate/marketdata/marketdata_fx_driver_rates_populate.sql's
   artefact seed block. Never hand-typed.

4. VERIFY
   Every row is independently verifiable two ways: (a) re-run step 1-3
   and diff -- the Fed's historical pages don't change once published, so
   the output is deterministic; (b) check manifest.json's sha256 for each
   page against a fresh download.

5. COMMIT
   git add external/fed_h10/ tools/fetch_fed_h10_rates.py \
       projects/ores.sql/populate/marketdata/marketdata_fx_driver_rates_populate.sql
   git commit -m "[sql] Regenerate FX driver rates from Fed H.10"

DATASET STRUCTURE
------------------
external/fed_h10/pages.zip
  - dat00_<code>.htm (one per currency, raw Fed H.10 page)

external/fed_h10/manifest.json
  - retrieved_at, the exact command that produced pages.zip, and one
    entry per page (code, pair, source_url, sha256, size_bytes) -- the
    single manifest describing the archive, kept beside it, not inside
    it.

projects/ores.sql/populate/marketdata/marketdata_fx_driver_rates_populate.sql
  - Registers the marketdata.fx_driver_rates DQ dataset, methodology,
    catalog, and bundle membership.
  - Seeds ores_dq_market_data_observations_artefact_tbl with 25 vintages
    (2016) x 11 currency pairs = 275 rows, each individually citing its
    own source_url/retrieved_at.

CURRENCY CLASSIFICATION
-------------------------
Majors (dense direct-quote CRM tier): EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/CHF,
USD/JPY, USD/SEK, AUD/USD, USD/CAD, NZD/USD.

Exotics (USD-pivot-star-only CRM tier): USD/ZAR, USD/MXN, USD/INR.

VALUE INTEGRITY
-----------------
No value in this dataset is derived, computed, triangulated, or typed
by hand -- every row is that currency's own Fed H.10 page, its own row,
for its own target date. An earlier draft of this dataset's task
documentation briefly (and incorrectly) hand-summarised two of these
EM currencies' rates from a web-page-summarisation tool rather than the
raw HTML; those values were wrong (off by ~5-8%) and were replaced with
the script-parsed, HTML-verified values recorded here before this
dataset was ever published. Lesson: only the fetch/parse/generate
pipeline above is a trusted source for this dataset -- never a
free-text summary of a page, however produced.
